document_metadata.title: newPhasePlanProcess v3.3.1 - Technical Specification & Architecture
document_metadata.purpose: Comprehensive technical documentation describing the newPhasePlanProcess system architecture, components, workflows, and integration points for feature development
document_metadata.scope: Complete system specification covering data structures, validation gates, execution flow, and extension points

executive_summary.core_purpose: Generate, validate, and execute deterministic, mechanically-verifiable project plans with step-level contracts and evidence chains
key_capabilities: AI-executable plan generation from natural language requirements
Step-level contracts with inputs/outputs/invariants/preconditions/postconditions
Template-defined validation gates (20 template gates, extensible)
File manifest with conflict analysis and step-level file scoping
Cryptographic evidence chain linking all execution steps
Self-healing failure types with bounded retries and convergence detection (5 defaults)
Worktree isolation for parallel execution safety
Ground truth validation levels (L0-L5) with mechanical pass/fail
Metrics tracking at phase and step granularity
Deterministic execution: same input → same plan hash → byte-identical output
Planning hierarchy with system/module/component/execution packet decomposition
Pipeline boundary contract specifying planning-to-execution boundary
Pattern-first execution with task to pattern registry mappings
Mutation model for classifying file changes and decomposing workstreams
Task intake model for continuous or queued task processing
Traceability mappings across requirements, modules, components, and execution packets
Execution baseline capture and drift detection
Concurrency control policies and lifecycle state machine
Rollback plan and evidence requirements
Canonicalization contract for consistent artifact representation
Merge policy and platform profile for cross-platform support
Registered executor model with pattern-to-executor binding and config schema enforcement
Configuration schema validation for every executable pattern instance
Mandatory pattern creation before implementation when no matching pattern exists in registry
Behavior-binding proof chain from requirement to pattern to config to executor to evidence
Ad hoc implementation detection and rejection via GATE-CFG series
Authoritative referenced context artifact binding
Artifact intent declaration before execution
Constrained pre-execution registry projection
Planned-vs-observed artifact reconciliation
Planned-vs-observed registry reconciliation
Contract-first abstraction governance for all governed module, tool, path, executor, phase, transport, and registry mutation boundaries
Path-key indirection with registry-backed resolver enforcement and hardcoded-path index gates
Reusable program contract preventing bespoke execution machinery when Gate, Phase, Mutation, Transport, or Executor programs can express the work
Deterministic implementation behavior profiles for CLI/code-generation defaults and situational behavior resolution
Execution identity policy using phase-scoped step_id values and globally unique phase_step_key joins
Template-placeholder semantics that distinguish reusable scaffold documents from executable plan instances
Generated artifact rebuild discipline for inventory, indexes, and evidence outputs
design_principles: NO IMPLIED BEHAVIOR: Every rule must be expressible as config + command + evidence + pass/fail
MECHANICAL VERIFICATION: All validation automated via executable commands and regex patterns
SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: No duplicate IDs, canonical derivation rules
EXPLICIT CONTRACTS: Every step declares inputs, outputs, pre/post conditions
AUDIT TRAIL: Cryptographic evidence chain for all execution steps
BOUNDED AUTOMATION: Self-healing with hard limits and escalation paths

CONFIGURATION-DRIVEN DEVELOPMENT: Executable behavior must be determined by approved external specifications and configurations consumed by registered executors; ad hoc imperative task logic is prohibited by default
DECLARED CONTEXT ONLY: implementation may rely only on plan-bound context artifacts
ARTIFACT INTENT BEFORE EXECUTION: every planned file mutation must be declared before execution
INTENT IS NOT REALITY: planned metadata must remain distinct from observed execution facts
MINIMAL PREDECLARATION: only stable pre-execution-known metadata may be projected into registry planning
CONTRACTS ARE ABSTRACTIONS: governed boundaries must communicate through versioned contracts, path keys, envelopes, and registered executors rather than raw calls or prose
PATHS ARE SEMANTIC KEYS: important/shared repo resources must be referenced by stable path keys and resolved through the path registry
REUSE BEFORE MACHINERY: new behavior should be expressed as specs/configs/schemas/tests before adding new runners, validators, or orchestration scripts
PHASE-SCOPED STEP IDENTITY: step_id is local to a phase; phase_step_key is the global execution join key
GENERATED ARTIFACTS ARE PROJECTIONS: inventory, indexes, and evidence must be rebuilt from authoritative sources, not treated as hand-authored state
primary_use_cases: AI-driven software project planning and execution

layer_1_planning.name: Planning Layer
layer_1_planning.purpose: Transform user requirements into validated, executable plans

components.name: Plan Template v3.3.0
components.role: Master template defining complete plan structure

components.name: Plan Schema v3.0.0
components.role: JSON Schema defining validation rules for plans
validation_scope: ID pattern enforcement (PLAN-*, PH-*, STEP-*, ART-*)

components.name: AI Agent Instructions
components.role: Complete guide for AI agents to generate valid plans

components.name: Pattern Registry
components.role: Single source of truth for approved execution patterns and their registry bindings

components.name: Executor Registry
components.role: Registry of approved executors and the pattern/config surfaces they support

components.name: Pattern Creation Workflow
components.role: Mandatory workflow for creating and validating a new pattern package before implementation begins

layer_2_validation.name: Validation Layer
layer_2_validation.purpose: Mechanically verify plan correctness and completeness

commands.normalize: Normalize plan structure (sort arrays, build ID maps)
commands.run-gates: Execute all validation gates in dependency order

components.name: Validation Gate System
execution_phases: path abstraction and hardcoded-path control (GATE-PATH-001 to GATE-PATH-003)

gate_registry.name: No Obfuscation Check
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure no TODO/TBD/FIXME placeholders exist
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every field must have real, specific content
gate_registry.failure_impact: Plan is incomplete and cannot be executed

gate_registry.name: Schema Validation
gate_registry.purpose: Validate plan conforms to JSON Schema
checks: Required fields present
Data types correct
ID patterns valid
Enum values within allowed set

gate_registry.name: Gate Definitions Valid
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure all gates have executable commands and evidence paths

gate_registry.name: Step Contracts Complete
gate_registry.purpose: Verify every step has complete contract
required_fields: behavior_spec (pattern_id, pattern_version, spec_file, config_instance_file, config_schema_file, parameter_schema_file)
gate_registry.critical_rule: All 19 executable-step contract fields mandatory for every step
gate_registry.failure_impact: Step cannot be mechanically verified or safely executed

gate_registry.name: Assumptions Documented
gate_registry.purpose: Validate all assumptions have risk assessment and validation method

gate_registry.name: File Mutation Determinism
gate_registry.purpose: Verify file mutation plans declare deterministic execution methods, hash guards, and evidence paths
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every planned file mutation must declare a deterministic application method with before/after integrity checks
gate_registry.failure_impact: File mutation execution may become non-deterministic or unverifiable

gate_registry.name: Unit Tests Pass
gate_registry.purpose: Verify all unit tests pass
gate_registry.critical_rule: All unit tests must pass before phase completion
gate_registry.failure_impact: Code quality cannot be verified

gate_registry.name: Coverage Threshold
gate_registry.purpose: Verify test coverage meets minimum threshold
checks: Coverage >= 80%
gate_registry.critical_rule: Test coverage must meet project standards
gate_registry.failure_impact: Insufficient test coverage creates reliability risk

gate_registry.name: Automation Spec
gate_registry.purpose: Validate automation configuration

gate_registry.name: Automation Index
gate_registry.purpose: Build searchable index of automation elements

gate_registry.name: Automation Diagrams
gate_registry.purpose: Generate DAG visualization of phase dependencies

gate_registry.name: Wiring Proof
gate_registry.purpose: Validate artifact flow integrity
checks: All artifacts have producers
All artifacts have consumers (except final deliverables)
No circular artifact dependencies

gate_registry.name: Artifact Registry
gate_registry.purpose: Validate artifact metadata completeness

gate_registry.name: Failure Modes Audit
gate_registry.purpose: Enumerate and validate all failure modes

gate_registry.name: E2E Wiring Validation
gate_registry.purpose: End-to-end artifact flow validation

gate_registry.name: Artifact Closure
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure all artifacts close (no orphans or dead ends)
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every input needs producer, every output needs consumer
gate_registry.failure_impact: Broken data flow, missing dependencies

gate_registry.name: Rollback Completeness
gate_registry.purpose: Verify every step has rollback procedure
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every step must be reversible
gate_registry.failure_impact: Cannot safely undo failed steps

gate_registry.name: Verification Completeness
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure all steps have postconditions with evidence
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every step must have verification method
gate_registry.failure_impact: Cannot mechanically verify step success

gate_registry.name: Single Source of Truth
gate_registry.purpose: Verify no duplicate IDs (step_id, artifact_id, assumption_id)
gate_registry.critical_rule: All IDs must be globally unique
gate_registry.failure_impact: Ambiguous references, broken traceability

gate_registry.name: Pattern Completeness
gate_registry.purpose: Verify every executable step has a pattern binding, executor binding, and schema-backed behavior spec
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every executable step must declare implementation_mode, pattern_id, executor_binding, and behavior_spec with schema-backed artifacts
gate_registry.failure_impact: Execution can proceed without a complete validated pattern package

gate_registry.name: Engine-Spec Separation
gate_registry.purpose: Reject executable behavior that is encoded in prompt prose or bespoke step logic instead of approved spec/config artifacts
gate_registry.critical_rule: Behavior must come from approved external spec/config artifacts consumed by a registered executor
gate_registry.failure_impact: NO IMPLIED BEHAVIOR is violated and execution becomes non-deterministic

gate_registry.name: No Ad Hoc Execution
gate_registry.purpose: Reject bespoke execution paths when an approved registered pattern exists for the task kind
gate_registry.critical_rule: Ad hoc execution is forbidden when a registered pattern exists or can be created first
gate_registry.failure_impact: Execution bypasses guardrails and loses reproducibility

gate_registry.name: Registered Executor Only
gate_registry.purpose: Verify every executor binding resolves to an approved executor registry entry
gate_registry.critical_rule: All executor_binding.executor_id values must exist in executor_registry
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unapproved executors can run without validated capability boundaries

gate_registry.name: Pattern-First Gap Resolution
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure implementation work is blocked until a PATTERN_CREATION path exists when no matching pattern is available
gate_registry.critical_rule: PATTERN_EXECUTION steps may not be scheduled before required PATTERN_CREATION work is complete
gate_registry.failure_impact: Implementation can begin without a validated governing pattern

gate_registry.name: Behavior-Binding Proof
gate_registry.purpose: Verify executor, spec, config, output contract, and evidence are mechanically linked
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every executable step must provide behavior_binding_proof linking executor, spec, config, outputs, and evidence
gate_registry.failure_impact: Behavior cannot be audited or reproduced mechanically

gate_registry.name: Automation Audit
gate_registry.purpose: Meta-validation of automation completeness

gate_registry.name: Goal Reconciliation
gate_registry.purpose: Final validation that plan achieves stated objectives

gate_registry.name: Referenced Context Completeness
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure all externally required technical artifacts are declared, scoped, and resolvable.
gate_registry.critical_rule: External implementation context must be bound through referenced_context_artifacts.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unbound context can silently change implementation behavior.

gate_registry.name: Context Authority Resolution
gate_registry.purpose: Resolve conflicting context artifacts through declared authority precedence.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Authoritative conflicts must fail closed or be explicitly resolved.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Conflicting context can make plan execution ambiguous.

gate_registry.name: Artifact Intent Completeness
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure required create, edit, and remove artifacts are declared before execution.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Done-critical artifacts must have intent records before execution.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Execution can produce undeclared deliverables.

gate_registry.name: No Undeclared Mutation Intent
gate_registry.purpose: Compare step-level file mutations to artifact_intent_manifest.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Steps may not imply mutations missing from artifact intent.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Mutation scope becomes unverifiable.

gate_registry.name: Planned Registry Projection Validity
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure planned registry projection contains only allowed pre-execution-known fields.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Observed, derived, and runtime fields are forbidden in planned projections.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Registry metadata can be falsely recorded as fact.

gate_registry.name: Planned vs Observed Artifact Reconciliation
gate_registry.purpose: Compare actual outputs to artifact intent records after execution.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Observed artifacts must match or explicitly reconcile against planned intent.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Delivery evidence can diverge from the declared plan.

gate_registry.name: Planned Projection vs Observed Registry Reconciliation
gate_registry.purpose: Compare observed registry writes to planned registry projections.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Observed registry facts must reconcile before final commit.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Registry facts can drift from planned scope.

gate_registry.name: Abstraction Governance Valid
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure abstraction_governance exists, uses fail-closed semantics, declares mandatory boundary types, and defines scoped exceptions.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Contracts are mandatory abstractions at governed boundaries.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Boundary behavior may become raw, implicit, or untestable.

gate_registry.name: Step Abstraction Bindings Complete
gate_registry.purpose: Verify every governed step declares abstraction_binding with input/output contracts and evidence.
gate_registry.critical_rule: No governed step may rely on unstated module/tool/path contracts.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Module handoff and CLI/tool behavior may drift.

gate_registry.name: Governed Path Key Coverage
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure all governed paths in file_manifest, artifact_intent_manifest, and planned_registry_projection have path keys.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Important/shared repo resources must use stable path keys.
gate_registry.failure_impact: File moves can break scripts, prompts, plans, and automation.

gate_registry.name: Path Resolver Valid
gate_registry.purpose: Resolve all declared path keys through the configured resolver and fail on unknown keys.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Path keys must resolve through the path registry without fuzzy matching or fallback.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Path abstraction becomes advisory instead of enforceable.

gate_registry.name: No New Hardcoded Governed Paths
gate_registry.purpose: Use hardcoded-path index enforcement to block newly introduced governed literal paths.
gate_registry.critical_rule: New governed path literals must be registered and referenced by key.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Path abstraction erodes and file-load refactors become brittle.

gate_registry.name: Reusable Program Contract Valid
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure the plan uses reusable program types and blocks new bespoke execution machinery unless approved.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Feature work should add specs/configs/schemas/tests, not new machinery.
gate_registry.failure_impact: File count increases through duplicated runners, validators, parsers, and orchestration scripts.

gate_registry.name: Step Reusable Program Bindings Complete
gate_registry.purpose: Verify every automation step maps to Gate, Phase, Transport, Mutation, Executor, or approved exception.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every automatable step must use a reusable deterministic program type.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Execution logic fragments across special-case scripts.

gate_registry.name: Implementation Behavior Determinism
gate_registry.purpose: Ensure behavior profiles and situational rules resolve to deterministic implementation behavior.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Implementation behavior cannot be chosen implicitly by AI or prompt prose.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Generated code style, I/O, path handling, state handling, and CLI behavior drift.

gate_registry.name: Execution Identity Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: Verify phase_id, step_id, phase_step_key, and gate_execution_stage semantics are consistent.
gate_registry.critical_rule: phase_step_key is globally unique; step_id is phase-scoped; stage labels are not confused with concrete phase IDs.
gate_registry.failure_impact: MCP tools, traceability joins, and evidence paths can reference the wrong execution unit.

gate_registry.name: Index Eligibility Drift Check
gate_registry.purpose: Verify index generator classifies identity-bearing arrays as index_eligible and does not require whole-array replacement for semantic records.
gate_registry.critical_rule: artifact_intent_manifest.records and planned_registry_projection.records must be patchable by stable identity.
gate_registry.failure_impact: RFC-6902 patches become fragile and can corrupt array ordering or unrelated records.

gate_registry.name: Referenced Tooling Exists
gate_registry.purpose: Verify all scripts, validators, schemas, generator tools, and resolver tools referenced by the three v3.3 documents exist or are explicitly marked planned artifacts.
gate_registry.critical_rule: A governance document may not claim executable enforcement unless the referenced tooling exists or is declared in artifact_intent_manifest.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Execution readiness is overstated and gates become documentation-only.

gate_registry.name: Situational Determinism Contract Valid
gate_registry.purpose: situational_determinism_contract is present with fail-closed resolution_order.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Execution branching must follow a declared deterministic resolution order.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Ambiguous situational resolution produces non-reproducible execution.

gate_registry.name: Governance Precedence Valid
gate_registry.purpose: governance_precedence declares a non-empty precedence_levels list.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Rule conflicts must be resolved by declared precedence, not implicit ordering.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Conflicting rules produce non-deterministic governance outcomes.

gate_registry.name: Executor Routing Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: executor_routing_policy provides a non-empty routing_rules list.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Executor selection must follow a declared, auditable routing contract.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Arbitrary executor selection breaks traceability and reproducibility.

gate_registry.name: Hermetic Step Contract Valid
gate_registry.purpose: hermetic_step_contract declares isolation_requirements for reproducible execution.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Steps must declare all inputs, outputs, and environment pins.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Undeclared dependencies cause irreproducible step outputs.

gate_registry.name: Decision Logging Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: decision_logging_policy specifies at least one log_targets path.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Every consequential decision must be logged for audit and replay.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Missing decision logs prevent post-hoc audit and debugging.

gate_registry.name: Durable Execution Contract Valid
gate_registry.purpose: durable_execution_contract declares a checkpoint_policy for resumable runs.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Executions must be resumable from verified checkpoints after failure.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Non-durable execution loses progress and breaks audit continuity.

gate_registry.name: Array Mutation Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: array_mutation_policy bans positional array-index edits and requires identity-key mutations.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Array mutations must use stable identity keys, never positional indexes.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Positional array edits silently corrupt documents when order changes.

gate_registry.name: Path Resolution Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: path_resolution_policy references a resolver script for governed path lookups.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Governed paths must be resolved through the declared resolver, never hardcoded.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Hardcoded paths break when the filesystem layout changes.

gate_registry.name: Canonical Document Resolution Valid
gate_registry.purpose: canonical_document_resolution names the authoritative V3_3 files.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Only the declared canonical filenames are authoritative sources of truth.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Ambiguous document identity causes divergent governance state.

gate_registry.name: Command Object Schema Valid
gate_registry.purpose: command_object_schema declares required fields for executable commands.
gate_registry.critical_rule: All executable commands must declare shell, cwd, timeout, failure mode, and evidence path.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Under-specified commands cannot be deterministically replayed or audited.

gate_registry.name: Pattern Resolution Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: pattern_resolution_policy provides approved_patterns with fail-closed fallback.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Tasks without an approved pattern must fail closed, not improvise execution.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unapproved execution patterns bypass governance and audit controls.

gate_registry.name: Fallback Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: fallback_policy declares at least one authorized fallback outcome.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Fallback behavior must be pre-authorized; ad-hoc fallbacks are forbidden.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unauthorized fallbacks produce unauditable and potentially unsafe outcomes.

gate_registry.name: Permission Tiers Valid
gate_registry.purpose: permission_tiers declares the four capability tiers.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Operations must be classified into a declared permission tier before execution.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unclassified operations bypass capability controls.

gate_registry.name: Provenance Contract Valid
gate_registry.purpose: provenance_contract requires producer, sha256, and timestamp_utc on all outputs.
gate_registry.critical_rule: All artifact outputs must carry traceable provenance metadata.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Missing provenance breaks audit chains and prevents tamper detection.

gate_registry.name: Change Archival Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: change_archival_policy lists directories that must be archived before destructive changes.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Evidence and patch artifacts must be archived before any destructive operation.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unarchived evidence cannot support rollback or compliance review.

gate_registry.name: Affected Execution Policy Valid
gate_registry.purpose: affected_execution_policy references a dependency graph for affected-only gate execution.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Selective gate execution must be driven by a declared dependency graph.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Arbitrary gate skipping misses required checks and invalidates the evidence chain.

gate_registry.name: Policy As Code Valid
gate_registry.purpose: policy_as_code declares a machine-evaluable policy_engine and policy_dir.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Acceptance rules must be expressed as machine-evaluable policies over structured input.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Prose-only acceptance rules cannot be mechanically enforced.

gate_registry.name: GitHub Enforcement Mapping Valid
gate_registry.purpose: github_enforcement_mapping declares merge_protection binding NPP gates to branch rules.
gate_registry.critical_rule: NPP validation must be bound to GitHub merge protection before PR merges.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Unbound gates allow merges that bypass governance checks.

gate_registry.name: Tool Context Generation Valid
gate_registry.purpose: tool_context_generation declares output_files generated from the NPP source of truth.
gate_registry.critical_rule: Tool context files must be generated from the NPP source of truth, not hand-authored.
gate_registry.failure_impact: Manually authored tool context diverges from the authoritative governance documents.

components.name: Failure Mode Checker
components.template_inclusion: Not included in base template v3.0.0; add via extension points as needed
checks: FM-01: Missing producers (artifacts consumed but not produced)
FM-02: Orphan artifacts (artifacts produced but never consumed)
FM-03: Circular dependencies (A depends on B depends on A)
FM-04: Dead flows (artifact chains that never reach final deliverable)
FM-05: Dormant flows (artifacts defined but not in any step)
FM-06: Invalid handoffs (type mismatches between producer/consumer)
FM-07: Evidence bundle gaps (missing evidence files)
FM-08: Recovery policy violations (missing rollback for critical steps)
FM-09: Idempotency violations (non-rerunnable steps without guards)
FM-10: File scope violations (step accesses forbidden paths)
FM-11: Write conflicts (parallel steps writing same files)
FM-12: DAG cycle detection (phase dependency cycles)
FM-13: Timeout inconsistencies (steps with unrealistic timeouts)
FM-14: Contract contradictions (postcondition contradicts invariant)

components.name: Configuration Schema Validator
components.role: Validates config instances against registered pattern config schemas
checks: Approved config schema exists for every executable pattern
Config instance matches schema version
Parameter schema exists for declared pattern parameters

components.name: Behavior Binding Validator
components.role: Verifies executor, behavior spec, config, and output contracts are mechanically linked
checks: Executor exists in executor registry
Behavior spec references approved artifacts
Expected outputs map to an output contract reference
Evidence command proves executor/spec/config binding

components.name: CDD Enforcement Gates
components.role: GATE-CFG-001 through GATE-CFG-006 block execution until configuration-driven requirements are satisfied

validation_guarantees: Plan completeness: all required sections present
Contract completeness: all steps have full contracts
Artifact integrity: all data flows close
Reversibility: all steps can be rolled back
Verifiability: all steps have mechanical verification
Executability: all commands are valid shell syntax
Safety: all parallel operations are conflict-free
Configuration-driven enforcement: executable behavior is bound to approved spec/config artifacts
Executor integrity: all executor bindings resolve to registered supported executors
Pattern completeness: missing pattern packages block implementation until resolved

layer_3_execution.name: Execution Layer
layer_3_execution.purpose: Execute validated plans with evidence collection and self-healing

components.name: Worktree Isolation System
components.purpose: Enable parallel execution with conflict prevention
components.base_concept: Each parallel phase executes in isolated git worktree
safety_rules: Each worktree writes ONLY inside its own root
Shared artifacts written only by orchestrator after validation
No direct commits to base_ref; merge via squash-merge
Worktree must pass all validation gates before merge
Step-level file scopes enforced within worktree

components.name: Self-Healing System
components.purpose: Automatically recover from common failure types

layer_4_observability.name: Observability Layer
layer_4_observability.purpose: Track execution progress and collect metrics

root_structure.description: Template v3.3.0 top-level sections (plan instances follow this layout).

section_purpose_map.description: Purpose of each top-level template section.

sections.purpose: Versioning, format, and breaking-change notes for the template.
Non-negotiable rules (NO IMPLIED BEHAVIOR) that govern all plans.
Declare the fail-closed configuration-driven development model, allowed behavior sources, and validation guarantees that block ad hoc execution.
Define pattern-first resolution, pattern creation, manual exception policy, and enforcement gates before executable work is permitted.
Step-by-step guidance for filling out the template.
Descriptions of v3 features such as step contracts, evidence chains, and file scoping.
Original request capture, classification, and derived success criteria.
Assumptions, in-scope/out-of-scope boundaries, and definition of done.
Complete file inventory with read/write scope and conflict checks.
Phase structure, parallelization plan, timing estimates, and DAG validation.
Failure types, retry budgets, evidence capture, convergence detection, and escalation rules.
Parallel worktree configuration, lifecycle, and safety rules.
Validation rigor levels (L0-L5) with evidence and enforcement rules.
Per-phase contracts defining inputs, outputs, invariants, and failure modes.
Per-step contracts with pre/post checks, file scope, and evidence.
Executable validation gates with regex expectations and evidence paths.
Tracked metrics, targets, summaries, and validation reporting.
Required and optional environment dependencies and resource checks.
Summary of deliverables, determinism score, and gate outcomes.
Agent instructions for generating compliant plans.
Define nested planning levels across system, module, component and execution packet scopes to ensure hierarchical decomposition and planning completeness.
Specify outputs of the planning stage and inputs required by the execution stage, and define required validations before execution begins.
Map tasks to pattern definitions stored in a registry, enabling pattern-first execution and reducing variability across similar tasks.
Register approved executors and the pattern/config schema surfaces they support for deterministic execution.
Declare allowed mutation types (edit_existing, create_new, remove_existing) and define rules for decomposing modifications into independent workstreams.
(Optional) Define sources and policies for continuous or queued task intake and normalization to the canonical project plan.
Provide mappings from high-level requirements to modules, modules to components, and components to execution packets to ensure alignment across planning layers.
Define the baseline state of the system or repository before execution to enable drift detection and rollback.
Specify policies for global and group-based concurrency limits across phases and steps.
Provide procedures and commands to revert changes at phase and step levels in case of irrecoverable failures.
Declare required evidence artifacts for validating success of phases and steps.
Enumerate allowed lifecycle states for tasks and artifacts (e.g., pending, in_progress, completed, failed, rolled_back).
Define allowed and disallowed transitions between lifecycle states.
List states used during drift reconciliation between planned and actual system state.
Define rules for ensuring canonical form of artifacts and results across environments.
Describe how worktrees or branches are merged back into the base branch, including strategy and review requirements.
Specify OS support and platform-specific configuration for commands and path separators.
Global list of file paths or patterns that must never be modified by any step. Enforced at runtime and verified post-run.
Containment policy ensuring all artifacts produced during a run are stored under a run-specific root directory, with leak detection.
Registry of known failure anti-patterns (hallucinated success, empty artifacts, planning loops, worktree contamination) with trigger conditions and corrective actions.
Schema and coherence rules for the run_status.json file that tracks execution progress and validates phase/task counts against reality.
State machine for tracking each patch through its full lifecycle (created → validated → queued → applied → verified → committed or rolled_back). Requires atomic, locked updates.
Bind external implementation context artifacts and declare their usage and authority.
Declare all intended file mutations and deliverables before execution.
Declare safe pre-execution-known registry metadata for planned artifacts.
Defines contract-first abstraction law, governed boundary types, required contract surfaces, and exceptions that prevent over-abstraction.
Declares path registry, resolver, governed path classes, hardcoded-path index policy, and fail-closed path-key enforcement.
Defines reusable program taxonomy and blocks new bespoke execution machinery unless a governed exception proves reuse is impossible.
Defines deterministic implementation defaults, behavior profiles, situational rules, and behavior binding validation for CLI/code-generation work.
Defines phase_id and step_id patterns, phase-scoped step identity, globally unique phase_step_key construction, and gate execution-stage separation.
Defines which blank/null scaffold values are allowed in the reusable template and forbids unresolved placeholders in concrete plan instances.

step_contract.description: Complete contract for a single execution step (template format)

purpose.description: Why this step exists and what it produces

item_structure.required: boolean - true if input must be present
item_structure.must_exist: boolean - true if input must exist before step

item_structure.required: boolean - true if output must be produced

invariants.description: Conditions that must remain true during step execution

preconditions.description: Executable shell checks run before the step (string commands)

postconditions.description: Executable shell checks run after the step (string commands)

required_fields.allowed_paths: Array of glob patterns for permitted access
required_fields.forbidden_paths: Array of glob patterns for denied access
required_fields.read_only_paths: Array of paths that cannot be modified

timeout_sec.description: Timeout in seconds for this step

pattern_id.description: Pattern identifier mapping this step to a registered execution pattern

pattern_parameters.description: Key/value parameters passed to the execution pattern

requires_manual_review.description: Indicates whether manual review is required before this step executes

manual_review_reason.description: Explanation for why manual review is required (e.g., modifies protected path)

high_risk_operation.description: If true, this step performs a high-risk operation requiring additional approvals or guards

required_fields.max_files_touched: number (nullable) - maximum files this step may modify
required_fields.max_file_size_mb: number (nullable) - maximum size of any single file modified

audit_fields.description: Configuration for audit trail record emission

required_fields.emit_audit_record: boolean - if true, emit an audit record for this step

failure_logging.description: Logging requirements for failure events at this step

required_fields.require_error_message: boolean - capture error message on failure
required_fields.require_stack_trace: boolean - capture stack trace on failure
required_fields.require_retry_history: boolean - log all retry attempts and outcomes

implementation_mode.description: Execution mode for the step. PATTERN_EXECUTION is the default for executable work; PATTERN_CREATION is used only to create a new pattern package; NOT_AUTOMATABLE is reserved for manual-only work.

executor_binding.description: Registered executor binding used to run this step mechanically

required_fields.executor_version: Version of the registered executor contract
required_fields.invocation_ref: Command, module path, or workflow reference used to invoke the executor

behavior_spec.description: Approved pattern and configuration artifacts that define the behavior surface for the step

required_fields.pattern_version: Version of the selected pattern contract
required_fields.spec_file: Pattern specification artifact that defines the behavior contract
required_fields.config_instance_file: Concrete config instance used for this step
required_fields.config_schema_file: Schema used to validate the config instance
required_fields.parameter_schema_file: Schema used to validate pattern parameters for this step

behavior_binding_proof.description: Mechanical proof that executor, pattern, config, and outputs are bound together

required_fields.command: Executable command that validates or materializes the binding
required_fields.evidence: Evidence artifact proving the binding check passed

allowed_extension_slots.description: Approved extension slots where bounded bespoke logic may execute

item_structure.slot_id: Named extension slot defined by the pattern
item_structure.description: Why the extension slot exists and what behavior it permits
item_structure.runtime_limits: Object describing time, file, or resource limits for logic inside the slot

bespoke_logic_allowed.description: If true, bespoke logic is permitted only inside approved allowed_extension_slots

artifact_definition.description: Optional metadata for a file or data artifact (not present in base template v3.0.0)

structure.name: Human-readable name
structure.producer_step: Step ID that creates this artifact
structure.consumer_steps: Array of step IDs that consume this artifact

structure.name: Human-readable name
structure.parallel_safe: boolean - true if can run parallel with other phases

referenced_context_artifacts.authority_semantics: authority_level controls whether an artifact is authoritative, constraining, or informational.
referenced_context_artifacts.scope_semantics: Each artifact declares the requirements, modules, components, and steps it applies to.
referenced_context_artifacts.conflict_resolution_semantics: Higher authority prevails when artifacts conflict; unresolved authoritative conflicts fail validation.
referenced_context_artifacts.usage_semantics: Steps depending on external context must reference the applicable context_artifact_id.

artifact_intent_manifest.difference_from_file_manifest: artifact_intent_manifest enumerates intended file mutations and deliverables; file_manifest remains the broader scope inventory.
artifact_intent_manifest.done_linkage: Each record states whether the artifact is required for definition of done and which validations apply.
artifact_intent_manifest.traceability: Records link to requirements, modules, components, and steps before execution begins.

planned_registry_projection.allowed_field_classes: Only fields that are stable and knowable before execution may be projected.
planned_registry_projection.forbidden_field_classes: Runtime observations, content hashes, actual timestamps, derived memberships, and computed rollups are forbidden before execution.
planned_registry_projection.reconciliation_rules: After execution, projected values are compared to observed registry facts and mismatches must be reconciled or explained.

abstraction_governance.description: Top-level governance section requiring versioned contracts at all governed boundaries.

path_abstraction_contract.description: Top-level governance section requiring path keys and resolver-backed path resolution for governed resources.

reusable_program_contract.description: Top-level governance section that makes reusable deterministic program types mandatory before new bespoke execution machinery.

implementation_behavior_contract.description: Top-level governance section for deterministic code-generation and CLI implementation defaults.

execution_identity_policy.description: Canonical identity policy for executable steps and gate bindings.
rules: step_id values are unique within a phase_id, not necessarily globally unique alone.
phase_step_key = {phase_id}/{step_id} is the global join key for execution, traceability, evidence, and MCP requests.
gate_execution_stage should represent PRE-EXECUTION/CORE-VALIDATION/POST-EXECUTION stage labels rather than overloading phase_id when no concrete phase applies.
Validators must detect duplicate phase_step_key values and duplicate step_id values within the same phase_id.

file_mutation_set.description: Planning output declaring all file changes
file_mutation_set.purpose: Enable registry integration and change impact analysis

structure.chain_valid: boolean - true if chain is unbroken

planning_hierarchy.description: Defines nested planning levels and their contents.

structure.system_level: High-level description of entire program/application, including mission and overall capabilities.
structure.module_level: Array of modules (or services) with their responsibilities, interfaces and boundaries.
structure.component_level: Array of components within modules, describing feature-level breakdown.
structure.execution_packet_level: Array of execution packets that map implementation tasks to components.

planning_hierarchy.purpose: Provide multi-level decomposition of the project to ensure planning completeness and traceability.

pipeline_boundary_contract.description: Formal contract between planning and execution stages.

structure.planning_stage_output: Path or identifier of the canonical plan produced by planning.
structure.execution_stage_input: Path or identifier of the plan consumed by execution stage.
structure.validation_required_before_execution: List of validation gates or scripts that must pass before execution begins.
structure.input_exclusivity_rule: Rule ensuring that only the specified plan document may be used as input for execution.
structure.allowed_execution_inputs: Array of the only permitted execution inputs (plan_json, registered_pattern_spec, schema_validated_config).
structure.forbid_direct_source_mutation_without_pa: Boolean blocking direct source mutation unless a validated pattern authorizes it.
structure.forbid_unregistered_executor_invocation: Boolean blocking executor calls unless the executor is registered and approved.
structure.forbid_runtime_behavior_override: Boolean blocking runtime prompt or command overrides of guardrailed behavior.

pipeline_boundary_contract.purpose: Ensure that plans are validated, pattern-bound, and immutable when handed off to execution.

execution_patterns.description: Mapping of task kinds to reusable pattern definitions stored in a registry.

structure.pattern_registry_reference: Identifier or path to the pattern registry to be consulted.
structure.required_for_task_kinds: Boolean indicating whether pattern mapping is mandatory for all task kinds.
structure.task_pattern_mappings: Array mapping task kinds to pattern IDs and optional constraints.

pattern_validation.require_pattern_id_per_step: Boolean indicating whether every executable step must reference a non-null pattern ID.
pattern_validation.required_pattern_components: Array of components every registered pattern must define (spec_file, config_schema_file, parameter_schema_file, executor_id, pattern_index_entry, guardrails_block, output_contract_ref, golden_test_fixture).
pattern_validation.validation_command: Command validating the pattern registry and per-step bindings.
pattern_validation.expect_exit_code: Expected exit code from pattern validation command.
pattern_validation.require_executor_binding_per_pattern: Boolean requiring every executable pattern to bind to a registered executor.
pattern_validation.require_behavior_binding_proof: Boolean requiring every executable step to provide mechanical behavior-binding proof.
pattern_validation.missing_pattern_action: Action when no matching pattern exists (fail_closed_create_pattern_task).

guardrail_policy.bypass_allowed: boolean - whether guardrails may be bypassed (must remain false)
guardrail_policy.runtime_override_allowed: boolean - whether runtime overrides are permitted (must remain false)
guardrail_policy.missing_guardrails_action: Action when a referenced pattern has no guardrails (fail_closed)
guardrail_policy.ad_hoc_execution_action: Action when a task attempts freeform execution outside a registered pattern (fail_closed_create_pattern_task)

execution_patterns.purpose: Reduce variability and enforce consistent execution by requiring validated pattern packages, registered executors, and fail-closed guardrails.

mutation_model.description: Declares allowed mutation types and rules for decomposing modifications into independent workstreams.

structure.allowed_mutation_types: Enumeration of permissible file mutation operations (edit_existing, create_new, remove_existing).
structure.workstream_decomposition_rules: Rules for breaking down large mutation sets into independent workstreams.
structure.independence_criteria: Criteria for determining when workstreams can be executed in parallel without conflicts.

mutation_model.purpose: Provide a deterministic model for file mutations and parallel workstream planning.

task_intake_model.description: Optional structure for continuous or queued task intake and normalization.

structure.sources: Sources from which new tasks or issues are collected (e.g., user requests, automated detections).
structure.normalization_target: Format into which tasks are normalized (typically project-specific JSON plan).
structure.queueing_policy: Policy governing how tasks are queued for planning.
structure.deduplication_policy: Rules to remove duplicates before processing.
structure.prioritization_policy: Rules for ordering tasks based on priority.

task_intake_model.purpose: Allow the system to operate autonomously by continuously ingesting and normalizing new work items.

traceability.description: Mappings that link planning levels to ensure end-to-end requirement satisfaction.

structure.requirement_to_module: Array of mappings from high-level requirements to modules that implement them.
structure.module_to_component: Array of mappings from modules to components.
structure.component_to_execution_packet: Array of mappings from components to execution packets (steps or tasks).
structure.gap_to_execution_packet: Array of mappings from identified gaps to the steps or tasks that close them.
structure.deferred_gaps: Array of unresolved gaps with explicit deferral justifications.
structure.gap_closure_validation: Validation configuration ensuring every gap is resolved or explicitly deferred.
structure.requirement_to_pattern: Array of mappings from requirements to the patterns that satisfy them.
structure.component_to_pattern: Array of mappings from implementation components to the patterns they rely on.
structure.step_to_executor: Array of mappings from executable steps to registered executor IDs.
structure.pattern_to_config_schema: Array of mappings from patterns to the config schemas that validate their behavior surfaces.
structure.pattern_to_output_contract: Array of mappings from patterns to the output contracts used to validate produced artifacts.

traceability.purpose: Maintain traceability from requirements through patterns, executors, and implementation artifacts, enabling verification and auditing.

worktree_isolation.description: Configuration for isolated Git worktrees used during parallel execution to prevent contamination.

structure.enabled: boolean - enable or disable worktree isolation
structure.base_ref: String - base branch for worktree creation

preflight.clean_base_branch_required: boolean - require base branch to be clean before worktree creation
preflight.base_branch_name: String - name of base branch expected to be clean
preflight.clean_check_commands: Array of commands verifying base branch cleanliness
preflight.on_dirty_action: String - action when base is dirty (fail_or_require_manual_cleanup)
preflight.forbid_untracked_sensitive_files: boolean - reject untracked sensitive files in base branch

post_run_verification.verify_base_ref_unchanged_before_merge: boolean - confirm base ref unchanged since worktree creation
post_run_verification.verify_no_direct_writes_to_base_ref: boolean - confirm no direct writes to base branch occurred

worktree_isolation.purpose: Ensure safe parallel isolation via separate Git worktrees with clean base-state entry and post-run verification.

execution_baseline.description: Capture baseline state of the repository before execution for drift detection and rollback reference.

git_state.required_clean: boolean - require repository to be clean at baseline
git_state.must_match_base_ref: boolean - require HEAD to match base_ref
git_state.check_commands: Array of commands checking current git state

artifact_root_baseline.allowed_roots: Array of allowed root directories for artifacts (.state/, .worktrees/)

execution_baseline.purpose: Provide reproducible baseline for drift detection, environment verification, and rollback support.

rollback_plan.description: Procedures to undo changes when failures occur at global, phase and step levels.

structure.per_phase: Object mapping phase IDs to rollback procedures
structure.per_step: Object mapping step IDs to rollback definitions
structure.failure_mode_to_recovery_mapping: Array mapping failure mode IDs to one or more recovery patterns
structure.fail_if_no_recovery_path: boolean - fail plan generation if any failure mode lacks a recovery path

rollback_plan.purpose: Ensure reliable, safe restoration from failures with explicit recovery paths for every known failure mode.

evidence_requirements.description: Required evidence artifacts for validating success of phases and steps.

structure.by_phase: Object mapping phase IDs to required evidence file arrays
structure.by_step: Object mapping step IDs to required evidence file arrays

audit_trail.required_fields: Array of fields required in each audit record

observable_success_requirements.require_exit_code: boolean - exit code must be captured
observable_success_requirements.require_expected_files: boolean - expected files must exist post-execution
observable_success_requirements.require_ledger_update: boolean - mutation ledger must be updated to confirm success

evidence_requirements.purpose: Ensure success is observable and auditable via mandatory evidence artifacts and audit trail records.

lifecycle_states.description: Lifecycle state machine for tasks and artifacts.

structure.states: Array of allowed task/artifact states (pending, in_progress, completed, failed, rolled_back)
structure.initial_state: String - starting state for all tasks
structure.run_status_states: Array of high-level run states (pending, running, blocked, failed, completed, rolled_back)
structure.mutation_ledger_states: Array of mutation lifecycle states (created, validated, queued, applied, verified, committed, rejected, failed, rolled_back)

lifecycle_states.purpose: Provide the state machine controlling task progression and patch lifecycle.

state_transitions.description: Allowed and forbidden transitions between lifecycle states.

structure.allowed: Array of {from, to} objects for valid task transitions
structure.run_status_allowed: Array of valid run-status state transitions
structure.mutation_ledger_allowed: Array of valid mutation-ledger state transitions
structure.lock_required_for_mutation_ledger: boolean - mutation ledger updates require a lock
structure.atomic_update_required: boolean - all state updates must be atomic

state_transitions.purpose: Enforce state coherence and prevent illegal transitions in tasks, run status, and mutation ledger.

merge_policy.description: Strategy and controls for merging worktrees back into the base branch.

structure.review_required: boolean - true if manual code review is required
structure.manual_review_triggers: Array of conditions requiring mandatory human review (bulk_rename_over_threshold, schema_change, pattern_addition, protected_path_request)
structure.approval_evidence_required: boolean - approval must be evidenced before merge proceeds

merge_policy.purpose: Enforce merge discipline including human review gates for high-risk operations.

protected_paths.description: Global protected file paths that cannot be modified by any step.

structure.paths: Array of glob patterns identifying protected files or directories

enforcement.runtime_block: boolean - halt execution on protected path violation
enforcement.post_run_verification: boolean - verify no protected files were modified

protected_paths.purpose: Prevent modification of critical system files globally, beyond step-level forbidden_paths.

run_artifact_policy.description: Rules ensuring all runtime artifacts are stored under a canonical runtime root.

structure.run_root_pattern: String pattern for the canonical runtime root (e.g., .state/)
structure.all_artifacts_must_be_under_run_root: boolean - strict containment within the canonical runtime root
structure.leak_detection_command: Command detecting artifacts outside the run root
structure.on_leak_action: Action when artifacts leak (move_to_run_root_and_warn or fail)

run_artifact_policy.purpose: Ensure runtime artifact containment for isolation, auditing, and reproducibility.

anti_pattern_detection.description: Configuration for detecting common failure anti-patterns and specifying remediation.

structure.enabled: boolean - enable or disable the detection system
structure.triggers: Array of trigger identifiers (hallucinated_success, planning_loop_over_threshold, empty_implementation_artifact, complexity_limit_exceeded, worktree_contamination, patternless_execution, null_pattern_id_on_executable_step, unregistered_executor_invocation, inline_behavior_logic_outside_extension_slot, runtime_override_of_guardrailed_parameters, spec_without_engine_binding, executor_without_config_schema, freehand_script_created_where_pattern_exists)
structure.checkpoints: Array of pipeline checkpoints (post_task, post_phase, pre_commit)
structure.actions: Object mapping severity levels (critical, recoverable) to actions (halt, self_heal_or_escalate)

anti_pattern_detection.purpose: Detect and respond to known failure anti-patterns early, including patternless or unregistered execution, to maintain plan integrity.

run_status_contract.description: Schema and validation rules for the run_status.json file tracking execution progress.

structure.required_fields: Array of required status fields (run_id, current_phase, phase_counts, task_counts, status, timestamps)
structure.coherence_checks: Validation rules ensuring counts and status match actual execution state
structure.validation_command: Command validating run status against plan and runtime
structure.expect_exit_code: Expected exit code from validation command

run_status_contract.purpose: Ensure accurate, coherent tracking of execution progress and detect inconsistencies early.

mutation_ledger.description: State machine for tracking every patch through its full lifecycle.

structure.states: Array of allowed ledger states (created, validated, queued, applied, verified, committed, rejected, failed, rolled_back)
structure.allowed_transitions: Array of {from, to} objects representing valid state transitions
structure.atomic_update_required: boolean - ledger updates must be atomic
structure.lock_required: boolean - ledger updates require a lock
structure.expect_exit_code: Expected exit code from validation command

mutation_ledger.purpose: Ensure tamper-proof tracking of patch states and enforce consistent progress through the change pipeline.

generated_artifact_policy.description: inventory.json, indexes, and evidence reports are generated projections and must be rebuilt from authoritative source documents.

plan_generation_workflow.description: Generate a new plan from requirements

outputs: Understanding of template structure and rules

tasks: Break phases into steps with unique step_ids

outputs: Complete step contracts for all steps

tasks: Verify every input has a producer
Verify every output has a consumer (or is final deliverable)

checks: No TODO/TBD/FIXME anywhere
All IDs unique (step_id, artifact_id, phase_id)
All executable steps have the full 19-field executable-step contract
All commands are executable shell syntax
All artifact flows close
No circular dependencies
All evidence paths follow pattern
All required pattern and executor bindings are present for executable work

steps.expected_result: All 27 validation gates pass + All 14 FM checks pass

success_criteria: Plan is executable without manual intervention

preconditions: Plan has passed all validation gates

substeps.on_failure: Skip phase and mark as blocked

outputs: Structured error report with remediation suggestions

validation_gate_execution_workflow.description: Execute all validation gates in dependency order

reconciliation_workflow.description: Workflow for keeping planned artifact and registry intent separate from observed execution facts until reconciliation completes.
steps: Declare minimal planned registry projection before execution.
Execute approved steps and collect observed outputs.
Commit observed registry facts only after reconciliation succeeds or records an explicit exception.

abstraction_governance_workflow.description: Resolve whether work crosses a governed boundary, bind the required contracts, validate abstraction bindings, and block execution if any governed boundary is raw or implicit.
steps: bind_path_keys_when logical resources are physical paths

path_abstraction_workflow.description: Convert governed physical paths into semantic keys, resolve them through the path registry, and reject new governed hard-coded literals.
steps: classify path as governed or ungoverned
resolve key through the declared resolver CLI
scan hardcoded path index for new violations

reusable_program_workflow.description: Classify automatable work into reusable program type before allowing new code files.
steps: classify work as Gate, Phase, Transport, Mutation, Executor, or non-automatable
create or update spec/config when runner exists
require exception package before new runner machinery

flow.action: Registry ingestion pipeline processes mutation set

benefits: Explicit declaration of all file changes

requirements: No TTY-dependent operations
All inputs via config files or environment variables
Timeouts on all blocking operations
Structured output (JSON/JSONL) for parsing
Exit codes follow conventions (0=success, non-zero=failure)

adding_new_validation_gates.description: How to add a new validation gate

requirements: Accept plan file path as argument
Return 0 on success, non-zero on failure
Output structured defects to stdout (JSON)
Generate evidence file in .state/evidence/

steps.add: Documentation and examples for new gate

commands: Create test plan with gate-specific scenarios
Run gate validator on test plan

adding_new_failure_modes.description: How to add a new failure mode check

requirements: Accept plan file path as argument
Detect specific failure pattern
Return 0 if no failures, non-zero if failures found
Output structured failure report

steps.add: Import and call new failure mode check

adding_new_self_healing_types.description: How to add a new self-healing failure type

requirements: Failure must be deterministic (detectable via regex)
Remediation must be automated (no human input)
Recovery success must be verifiable

adding_new_ground_truth_levels.description: How to extend ground truth validation

configuration_driven_development.description: Normative configuration-driven development contract for executable behavior.
configuration_driven_development.definition: Configuration-Driven Development requires all automatable behavior to be expressed through approved external pattern specifications, config schemas, and parameter schemas consumed by registered executors.
configuration_driven_development.core_principle: Behavior belongs to validated specification and configuration artifacts; implementation code supplies the engine surface and approved extension slots only.

mandatory_separation.engine: Registered executors provide reusable mechanics and must not encode task-specific behavior directly.
mandatory_separation.behavior_surface: Pattern specs, config instances, config schemas, and parameter schemas define task-specific behavior.
mandatory_separation.extension_surface: Bounded extension slots are the only place bespoke logic may exist, with explicit runtime limits and evidence requirements.

glossary.executor: A registered execution engine identified by executor_id and constrained by declared supported surfaces.
glossary.pattern: An approved reusable behavior contract that maps a task kind to spec, schemas, outputs, and guardrails.
glossary.config_schema: The schema used to validate configuration instances consumed by a pattern.
glossary.parameter_schema: The schema used to validate pattern parameters bound at planning time.
glossary.behavior_binding_proof: Mechanical evidence linking executor, pattern, config, and expected outputs for a step.
glossary.extension_slot: A named and bounded location where bespoke logic may execute under explicit limits.
glossary.pattern_creation_step: A step whose sole purpose is to create and validate a new pattern package before implementation.
glossary.executable_step: A step scheduled to run through a registered executor under PATTERN_EXECUTION mode.
glossary.ad_hoc_logic: Task-specific imperative behavior not sourced from an approved pattern/config surface.
glossary.variance_path: An approved manual exception path requiring explicit human authorization and expiration.

validation_guarantees: Every executable step is bound to a validated pattern package before execution.
Every config instance is validated against an approved config schema.
Every executor binding resolves to a registered executor with declared supported surfaces.
Every behavior binding produces evidence that links executor, pattern, config, and outputs.
Pattern gaps fail closed until pattern creation and validation complete.
configuration_driven_development.purpose: Make executable behavior deterministic, auditable, and fail-closed by separating engine logic from approved behavior artifacts.

pattern_governance.description: Pattern-first governance rules that must be satisfied before executable work is planned or run.
pattern_governance.purpose: Force pattern resolution, pattern creation, and registered executor binding to complete before implementation work is planned or executed.

executor_registry.description: Specification for the registered executor catalog used by pattern-bound steps.

registered_executors.executor_entrypoint: Command, module path, or workflow reference used to invoke the executor
registered_executors.supported_pattern_ids: Array of pattern IDs the executor may run
registered_executors.supported_config_schema_versions: Array of config schema versions supported by the executor
registered_executors.supported_parameter_schema_versions: Array of parameter schema versions supported by the executor
registered_executors.output_contract_refs: Array of output contract references validated for this executor
registered_executors.determinism_requirements: Array of deterministic execution constraints the executor must satisfy
registered_executors.forbidden_capabilities: Array of capabilities the executor is prohibited from performing

structure.validation_command: Command validating executor registry completeness and cross-file bindings
structure.expect_exit_code: Expected exit code from executor registry validation command

executor_registry.purpose: Ensure every executable step binds only to a registered executor with validated pattern, config, and output contract compatibility.

adding_new_features.principle: Features must preserve the configuration-driven, pattern-first execution contract
requirements: Feature must be expressible as: registered executor + validated spec/config artifact + evidence artifact + pass/fail condition
If feature cannot be automated, it must be marked NOT_AUTOMATABLE and routed to manual process
Feature must not break existing validation gates
Feature must include evidence collection
Feature must include rollback procedure
Feature must update schema, template, and AI instructions
If no pattern exists, first create a pattern package (spec, config schema, parameter schema, executor binding, golden fixture) and validate it
No plan may schedule PATTERN_EXECUTION steps against a task kind lacking a validated registered pattern
Spec-only pattern definitions are invalid unless a corresponding executor binding exists in executor_registry

workflow.create: New validator script or extend existing
workflow.register: In gate dependency graph if new gate

workflow.verify: All gates pass with new feature

forward_compatibility.rule: Future versions must not break v3.0.0 validation
forward_compatibility.mechanism: Schema versioning and additive changes only

extension_compatibility.rule: Extensions must not require core system changes
extension_compatibility.mechanism: Plugin architecture via external validators and custom gates

integration_tests.scope: Gate execution sequences and evidence chain validation

end_to_end_tests.scope: Full plan lifecycle from generation to execution
end_to_end_tests.method: Create real plans for test projects
end_to_end_tests.validation: All gates pass and execution completes successfully

key_files.NEWPHASEPLANPROCESS_INSTRUCTION_DOCUMENT: AI agent guide for plan generation

scripts/.purpose: Executable scripts for validation and execution

wiring: wiring/ subdirectory with 14 failure mode checkers

.state/.purpose: Canonical runtime root for state, evidence, metrics, and per-run artifacts

structure.evidence/: Evidence files organized by phase and step
structure.planning/: Planning artifacts and file mutation sets

terms.definition: Complete specification of a single execution step including inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, commands, rollback, evidence, and idempotency configuration
Property ensuring every artifact input has a producer and every artifact output has a consumer (or is a final deliverable), with no orphans or dead ends
Automated check that validates a specific aspect of plan correctness, executed via script with pass/fail result
Specific pattern of plan defect detectable via automated check (e.g., circular dependency, missing producer)
Cryptographically-linked sequence of execution proofs showing what was done, when, and with what result
Degree of validation rigor (L0-L5) ranging from documentation-only to production-like validation
Technique using git worktrees to enable parallel phase execution without file conflicts
Automated recovery from common failure types with bounded retries and convergence detection
Property of a step that allows safe re-execution with same result
Directed Acyclic Graph defining phase dependencies and execution order
Explicit declaration of all file creations, modifications, and deletions planned by a project
Core design principle requiring all rules to be expressible as config + command + evidence + pass/fail
A structured decomposition of planning across multiple scope levels (system, module, component, execution packet) to improve comprehensiveness and traceability.
Formal specification of the outputs from the planning stage and the inputs required by the execution stage, including required validations before execution.
A reusable contract-like template stored in a registry that defines how a particular type of task should be executed.
Model specifying allowed file mutation operations and guidelines for decomposing them into independent workstreams.
Structure describing how new tasks are ingested, normalized, deduplicated, prioritized and queued for planning.
Mappings that link requirements to modules, modules to components, and components to execution steps to ensure end-to-end alignment and auditability.
Baseline system state captured before execution, including commit hash, dependencies and environment snapshot.
Policies that limit or coordinate parallel execution of phases and steps.
Set of procedures to revert changes when execution cannot proceed or must be undone.
List of artefacts required to demonstrate success of each phase and step.
Enumerated states representing progression of tasks and artefacts through the pipeline.
Allowed transitions between lifecycle states.
States used during drift detection and reconciliation.
Rules ensuring consistent representation of data and artefacts across environments.
Strategy and requirements for merging changes from worktrees into the main branch.
Definition of supported operating systems and platform-specific configuration.

references.title: Plan Template v3.3.0
references.description: Complete template with all features and examples

references.title: Plan Schema v3.0.0
references.description: JSON Schema defining plan validation rules

references.title: Instruction Document v3.3.0
references.description: Complete guide for AI-driven plan generation and plan-filling behavior

changes: Added executor_registry with registered executor binding
Added GATE-CFG-001 through GATE-CFG-006 configuration-driven validation gates
Added step-level implementation_mode, executor_binding, behavior_spec, behavior_binding_proof fields
Extended planning_hierarchy with implementation_style and engine_component_id
Added traceability mappings for requirement_to_pattern, component_to_pattern, step_to_executor
Mandatory pattern creation before implementation when no matching pattern exists
Added step-level contracts with template-required fields
Added worktree isolation for parallel execution
Added template default validation gates; current release metadata supersedes early gate-count wording.
Added referenced_context_artifacts, artifact_intent_manifest, and planned_registry_projection sections.
Added context, artifact intent, registry projection, and reconciliation validation gates.
Aligned release metadata and template default gate inventory.

breaking_changes: Step contracts now mandatory (13 required fields)
step_id must be unique across all phases
Preconditions and postconditions must be executable shell syntax (not descriptions)

migration_steps.action: Add complete contracts to all steps
Convert pre/postcondition descriptions to executable commands
